title: Gnidia kerstingii
intro: Gnidia kerstingii, also known as the Yellow Milk Tree, is a succulent plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and milk-like latex. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gnidia kerstingii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and cardiac glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in water to make teas, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Gnidia kerstingii is classified within the family Oliniaceae. This family belongs to the order Myrtales, which includes several other families of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is a part of the larger clade Eurosids II in the APG III classification system. The clade Eurosids II is a subset of the larger clade Rosids, which itself is a subset of the superclade Pentapetaleae. The Pentapetaleae superclade is a subset of the larger clade Angiosperms.

The plant is said to have various medicinal uses, including treating wounds, fever, and other health issues.
